findings.title: Window rollover can erase samples recorded for the new window
findings.impact: At a window boundary, the winning thread first publishes the new window start and only then exchanges the shared counters to zero. A concurrent recorder can observe the new start, add its success/failure sample to the current-window counter, and then have that sample wiped by the settling thread's exchange. Under concurrent Set/Get traffic this makes the tracker compute incorrect sample counts and success rates, which can suppress or delay URMA failover even though the data plane is unhealthy.
findings.fix: Make rollover atomic with respect to counter updates, for example by protecting windowStartTimeMs_ and the counters with one small mutex, or by moving to per-window counter objects/generation-tagged counters so recorders cannot add to the new generation before the old generation is snapshotted and reset.
